canonical_definition: Based on the available data, LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations) is characterized as a feature attribution technique used to explain the individual predictions of a model. The provided source describes it as a "widely-used off-the-shelf" method that is "frequently applied within specific NLP applications." This framing suggests its common use as a practical tool for model interpretation. As an example of its application, one study is cited for using LIME to elucidate hate speech detection models, particularly in the context of low-resource languages like Urdu and Sindhi.
papers: How Do Large Vision-Language Models See Text in Image? Unveiling the Distinctive Role ofOCRHeads
Interpretable Mnemonic Generation for Kanji Learning via Expectation-Maximization
VoiceBBQ: Investigating Effect of Content and Acoustics in Social Bias of Spoken Language Model

definitions.text: Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) is a feature attribution technique used to explain individual predictions of a model.
